Title: Glass Ceiling
Speaker: Board Chair Okonkwo
Setting: 67th floor boardroom, panoramic windows, {location} at altitude
TextLines: Seven chairs. Five occupied. The empty two belong to members who voted against this hearing.
Okonkwo's face is a mask of corporate patience — the kind that costs six figures in executive coaching. The holographic minutes float between you.
"Your division's operational expenditure has exceeded projected returns by a factor of three. Your heat index is a liability the insurance underwriters have flagged. Twice."
A pause calibrated to the millisecond.
"Convince us you're still an asset and not a line item waiting for deletion."
Somewhere below, {operative.role:conscience} is probably updating their resume.

Choices.Label: "The numbers don't capture what we prevent. I have case files."
"Cut my budget in half. I'll deliver the same results."
"Fire me. See what happens to your containment metrics."
